response:
The Church at Hoxne was dedicated to Edmund the Martyr, who was the king of **East Anglia**.

This is directly stated in the context: "Hoxne manor... rededicated the church at Hoxne to honour Edmund the Martyr, and kept control of the Hoxne manor house, though himself locating elsewhere." Additionally, it is mentioned that "Edmund the Martyr (also known as St Edmund or Edmund of East Anglia, died 20 November 869) was king of East Anglia from about 855 until his death."
